display.purpose: Page titles; the heaviest type on any screen.

title.purpose: Card and post titles, section sub-heads.

body.purpose: Default text and help copy; cap prose at 65–75ch.

mono.purpose: Prompt editor, JSON sample data, IDs, RSS URLs.

shadows.name: hover-lift
shadows.purpose: Clickable cards on hover only — flat at rest.

shadows.name: floating-layer
shadows.purpose: Toasts and modal dialogs above the page.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The one high-emphasis action per view.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display:inline-flex; align-items:center; height:36px; padding:0 16px; font-family:Inter,system-ui,sans-serif; font-weight:600; font-size:13px; color:#ffffff; background:#a2391a; border:1px solid #a2391a; border-radius:0.5rem; cursor:pointer; transition:background 0.15s ease, border-color 0.15s ease; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background:#c3512f; border-color:#c3512f; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline:2px solid #a2391a; outline-offset:2px; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { display:inline-flex; align-items:center; height:36px; padding:0 16px; font-family:Inter,system-ui,sans-serif; font-weight:600; font-size:13px; color:#1b1c1c; background:#ffffff; border:1px solid #e4e2e2; border-radius:0.5rem; cursor:pointer; transition:background 0.15s ease, border-color 0.15s ease; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background:#f5f3f3; border-color:#dec0b8; }

components.name: Danger Button
components.css: .ds-btn-danger { display:inline-flex; align-items:center; height:30px; padding:0 10px; font-family:Inter,system-ui,sans-serif; font-weight:600; font-size:12px; color:#ba1a1a; background:#ffdad6; border:1px solid #ffdad6; border-radius:0.25rem; cursor:pointer; transition:background 0.15s ease, color 0.15s ease; } .ds-btn-danger:hover { background:#ba1a1a; color:#ffffff; border-color:#ba1a1a; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Single-line field; focus shows a terracotta ring.
components.css: .ds-input { width:260px; height:38px; padding:0 12px; font-family:Inter,system-ui,sans-serif; font-size:15px; color:#1b1c1c; background:#ffffff; border:1px solid #e4e2e2; border-radius:0.5rem; outline:none; transition:border-color 0.15s ease, box-shadow 0.15s ease; } .ds-input:focus { border-color:#a2391a; box-shadow:0 0 0 3px #ffdbd1; }

components.name: Active Nav Item
components.css: .ds-nav-active { display:flex; align-items:center; gap:10px; height:40px; padding:0 12px; font-family:Inter,system-ui,sans-serif; font-size:13px; font-weight:600; color:#a2391a; background:#ffdbd1; border-radius:0.5rem; text-decoration:none; } .ds-nav-active .ds-nav-ico { width:18px; text-align:center; opacity:0.9; }

components.name: Primary Badge
components.description: Status / tag pill in the accent's quiet form.
components.css: .ds-badge { display:inline-flex; align-items:center; padding:2px 8px; font-family:Inter,system-ui,sans-serif; font-size:11px; font-weight:600; letter-spacing:0.02em; color:#a2391a; background:#ffdbd1; border-radius:9999px; }

components.name: Content Card
components.description: Flat-at-rest container; lifts on hover when clickable.
components.css: .ds-card { padding:24px; background:#ffffff; color:#1b1c1c; border:1px solid #e4e2e2; border-radius:1rem; transition:box-shadow 0.15s ease; } .ds-card:hover { box-shadow:0 4px 16px rgba(0,0,0,0.07); }

narrative.overview: Newspapper is a focused editorial workstation: ink set on warm paper, with a single terracotta stamp standing in for the press. It is not a marketing surface and not a playful consumer app — it is the desk a person sits at, daily, to turn the day's news into a clean slide post. The visual system serves that task and then gets out of the way. Warmth comes from the paper-toned surface, the brown-tinted neutrals, and the one confident accent — never from gradients, glass, or ornament. Density is deliberate, and width is used intentionally: full-bleed where the work is, a narrow reading column where the content is prose.
keyCharacteristics: One terracotta accent (#a2391a) on a warm off-white (#fbf9f8); accent ≤ ~10% of any screen.
Inter only, 400–900; hierarchy from weight and size, not from font pairing.
Flat by default; a single soft shadow appears on hover.
Intentional width: full-bleed work areas, centered columns for prose and forms.

rules.name: The One Voice Rule
rules.body: Terracotta is the only accent. It appears on ≤ ~10% of any screen — primary action, current selection, brand mark, focus. Its rarity is what makes it read as confident rather than loud.

rules.name: The Warm Neutral Rule
rules.body: Every neutral is tinted toward the brand's brown hue. Never reach for a cool/blue-gray neutral; it breaks the paper feeling.

rules.name: The Weight-Not-Family Rule
rules.body: Contrast comes from Inter's weight range, never from pairing a second font.

rules.name: The Earned-Label Rule
rules.body: All-caps labels belong inside panels and tables, one per region. They are forbidden as decorative eyebrows above page sections.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest. A shadow is a response to state (hover) or a signal of layer (modal/toast) — never decoration on a resting element.

dos: Do keep terracotta to ≤ ~10% of a screen — primary action, current selection, brand, focus.
Do tint every neutral toward the brand's warm brown hue; use surface-low/container for tonal layering instead of shadow.
Do build type hierarchy from Inter's weight and size alone.
Do use width intentionally: full-bleed for work areas, a centered 720px column for forms/prose, a centered 1080px column for lists.
Do give every interactive element its full state set: default, hover, focus-visible, active, disabled, loading.
Do use skeletons for content loading, not a centered spinner.
donts: Don't add an eyebrow kicker / all-caps label above every page section.
Don't use border-left greater than 1px as a colored accent stripe; use a full 1px border + a leading icon.
Don't introduce a second saturated accent color, gradients, gradient text, or glassmorphism.
Don't ship the SaaS-cream dashboard look, the over-decorated tool, or narrow content marooned in empty space.
Don't nest cards inside cards, or apply a resting shadow as decoration.
Don't reach for a cool/blue-gray neutral; it breaks the warm paper feeling.
